steps.title: Track the question and answer
steps.content: The text opens by asking why sand cats purr but lions roar. It then presents researchers' hypothesis that the difference may be partly due to the hyoid bone and explains how the bone differs between the two species.

steps.title: Reject wrong structure claims
steps.content: Choice A says the facts weaken the theory, but the anatomical details support it. Choice B invents habitat discussion, and Choice C says the text describes shared behaviors even though it contrasts purring and roaring. The structure is a question followed by a possible answer. The answer is D.
